folio.label: 173r
folio.l: for they that folily wasten , & despenden the goodes æ æei han

whan they han nomoore ¸pre of here owne , they shapen he~
to take the goodes of a nother man ,

I seye tha~ne , that ©e shuln
flee auarice ,

vsynge ©oure richesses in swicú manere , æ men
seyn nat , æ ©oure richesses ben I buried ,

but æ ©e haue hem
in ©oure mygút , and in ©oure weldynge ,

For a wys man re-
päuetú the auaricious man , & seitú thus in two vers

¢ Where
to & why burietú a man hise goodes by his gret auarice , & know-
etú wel , æ nedes must he dye ,

For detú is the ende of euäy
man , as in this päsent lyf ,

And for what cause or enchesou~
ioynetú he hi~ , or knyttetú he hi~ so faste , vn to hise goodes ,

that alle hise wyttes moun nat disseuäen hi~ , or de¹ten hi~ from
hise goodes ,

And he knowetú wel , or ougúte knowe , æ whan he
is ded , he shal no thyngö bere w hi~ , out of æis world

¢ And æä-
fore seitú Seynt Austyn ; æ the Auaricious man is likned
vn to helle ,

æ æe moore it swolwetú , the moore desir it hatú to
swolwe & deuoure ,

And as wel as ©e wolde eschue to be called
an auaricious man or chynche ,

as wel shulde ©e kepe ©ow , and
gouäne ©ow in swicú a wyse , æ men calle ©ow nat Fool large

¢ Therfore seitú Tullius , the goodes of thyn hous , ne shulde
nat ben hid ne kept so cloos , but that they mygúte ben ope-
ned by pitee & debonairetee ,

æ is to seyn ; to ©eue he~ part
æ han gret nede ,

ne thyne goodes shulden nat ben so open
to be euäy mannes goodes

¢ Afterward in getyngö of ©ourö riches-
ses , and in vsynge of he~ , ©e shuln alwey haue thre thynges in
©oure herte ,
æ is to seyn ; oure lord god , Conscience , & good name ,
¢ First ©e shuln haue god in ©oure herte ,

& for no richesse ©e
shuln do no thyngö , whicú may in any manere displese god , æ
is ©oure creature & makere

¢ For after the word of Salamon ;
It is bettre to haue a litel good , w loue of god ,

than to haue
muchel good , & lese the loue of his lord god

¢ And the ¸phete
seitú ; æ bettre it is , to ben a good man , & haue litel good &
tresor ,
than to be holden a shrewe , & haue grete richesses ¢

And ©et I seye foræämore , æ ©e shulden alwey do ©oure bysynes-
se , to gete ©ow richesses ,
so æ ©e gete he~ w good conscience ,,

And the Apostle seitú ; æ there nys no no thyngö in this world
of whicú we shulden haue so gret ioye , as whan oure con-
science beretú vs good witnesse

¢ Afterward in getynge of ©ourö richesses ,
And in vsynge of he~ ,

©ow muste haue gret bisynesse , & gret
diligence , æ ©oure good name , be alwey kept & conserued ,,
¢ For Salamou~ seitú ; æ bettre it is , & moore it auailetú a
